hypothesis: A 25-record ExECT S4 v1.1 diagnostic cap tests whether dual-cardinal seizure frequency label policy and near-miss bridges improve frequency F1 while frozen S3 v1.2 families hold, without changing scorer semantics.
metric_caveats: This diagnostic validation cap is a gating check for ExECT S4 field expansion; it is not a performance estimate.
These are partial ExECT S4 diagnostics (eleven families), not published ExECTv2 Table 1 reproduction.
Pooled micro F1 across eleven families is not comparable to frozen S3 nine-family or S2 five-family headlines.
S3 label-policy bridges from frozen v1.2 are reused for S1–S3 families; see docs/exect_s4_gold_policy.md.
Cap-25 ExECT runs are systematically optimistic vs full validation (~7–9pp on S1); treat as schema/gate signal only.
Report per-family F1 and monitor frozen S3 families (diagnosis, seizure, medication, investigation, comorbidity, birth/onset/cause/when-diagnosed).
Evidence quote support should be reported separately from field-family precision, recall, and F1.
